title: Race-lap conditional logit over the active field
summary: A pit call is a choice made within a lap-level choice set, not an IID binary event.
rationale: A pit call is a choice made within a lap-level choice set, not an IID binary event. Conditional logit is designed for selecting one alternative from competing options, and a grouped choice model can capture why one driver pits now while others on the same lap do not.
implementation_hint: Reshape each (Year,Race,LapNumber) into a choice set. Train a conditional-logit-style model or grouped softmax scorer on driver-lap utilities built from current features plus within-lap centered versions. Compare against binary GBDT using race-blocked CV; optionally blend its probabilities with the baseline.
expected_effect: Better relative ordering within the same lap, especially in crowded pit windows where absolute tyre-age signals are similar across drivers.
risk: If many laps have zero or multiple pitters, the choice-set assumption is imperfect; needs careful handling of 'no pit this lap' and multi-pit laps.
